column1: # SOTA-Replay v1 — run bigbag's 2026-04-09 record script locally vs ours.
# WHY: bigbag's record (1.0810 BPB, 8xH100 x 600s) is in records/track_10min_16mb/
# 2026-04-09_... . Their train_gpt.py is LZMA-obfuscated but our train_gpt_sota_decoded.py
# is our DECODED version of the same lineage. So their script + our script share
# architecture; the differences are knob values:
#   THEIRS: QK=5.25, WARMDOWN=0.72, TTT_EPOCHS=3, EMA=0.9965, LOGIT_SOFTCAP=30 (default)
#   OURS:   QK=5.5,  WARMDOWN=0.64, TTT_EPOCHS=1, EMA=0.995,  LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20
# At submission scale they converge to 1.0810 vs our 1.1464 (gap 0.066).
# At 500 steps on Spark we cannot reach those absolute numbers — we're comparing DIRECTION.
# Hypotheses this sweep tests:
#   H1: THEIR_script + THEIR_knobs beats OUR_script + OUR_knobs at equal 500-step budget
#       → our decoded port has silently broken something
#   H2: OUR_knobs on THEIR_script beats THEIR_knobs on THEIR_script
#       → our knob claims (QK=5.5 etc.) actually travel; we should double down
#   H3: THEIR_knobs on OUR_script beats OUR_knobs on OUR_script
#       → our knob claims are overfit to our decoding; revert toward their values
# All rows seed=42 for alignment with their published train_seed42.log trajectory.
# Their record log shows step 4550 quantized_sliding_window val_bpb=1.08286 — we
# won't reach that at 500 steps, but the cross-comparison is the signal.
# Script path uses records/ folder which contains both train_gpt.py (their LZMA)
# and reference logs.
# SCRIPT= selects which training file run_experiment.sh invokes.
sota_replay_00_ours_ourknobs
column2: SCRIPT=train_gpt_sota_decoded.py QK_GAIN_INIT=5.5 WARMDOWN_FRAC=0.64 TTT_ENABLED=1 SLIDING_WINDOW_ENABLED=1 TTT_EPOCHS=1 EMA_DECAY=0.995 LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20 MATRIX_LR=0.042 ITERATIONS=500 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=1800 TIMEOUT_SECS=3600 FAST_SMOKE=0 SEED=42 NOTES=ours_ourknobs_control
